statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided in stdin as two separate lines. The first line will be the number of elements in array. The second line will be space-separated elements of the array.
statement.outputFormat: Output will be a boolean indicating whether the target is found in the array.

examples.explanation: Target 3 is found in the array
Target 100 is not found in the array

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums, int target) { int left = 0, right = nums.size() - 2; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) return true; else if (nums[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return false; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums, int target) { int left = 0, right = nums.length - 2; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) return true; else if (nums[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return false; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool: left, right = 0, len(nums) - 2 while left <= right: mid = left + (right - left) // 2 if nums[mid] == target: return True elif nums[mid] < target: left = mid + 1 else: right = mid - 1 return False
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums, target) { let left = 0, right = nums.length - 2; while (left <= right) { let mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) return true; else if (nums[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return false; }

functionSignature.python: bool solve(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool
